星期三，Linux基金会，旨在通过开源技术旨在实现大众创新的非营利组织，宣布推出Agstack基金会，这是专门为全球农业生态系统设计的第一个开源数字基础架构项目。
 Agstack Foundation将通过创建，维护和加强自由，可重复使用，开放和专业的数字基础设施来提高全球农业效率，用于农业数据和应用。
 创始成员和贡献者包括技术和农业产业，跨越行业和地理位置的领导者。会员和合作伙伴包括Agralogics，呼吁代码，中心全球，数字绿色，农场基金会，农场，HPE，IBM，混合碗和amp;更好的食品企业，NIAB，OpeneNeam，我们的SCI，生产营销协会，普渡大学/燕麦＆amp;农业信息实验室，加州大学农业和自然资源（UC-ANR）和加州大学圣巴巴拉Smartfarm项目。
 “全球农业生态系统拼命地需要数字化改造。由于没有可重复使用的工具和数据，由于没有可重复使用的工具和数据，生产力和创新存在太多。我很高兴引领领导者，贡献者和成员的社会 - 来自各界和国家 - 帮助建立这种共同和可重复使用的资源 -  Agstack  - 这将有助于全球农业的每个利益攸关方，并使用自由和开放的数字工具和数据，“Ampstack执行董事Sumer Johal说。
 Linux基金会在周三官方的Agstack公告中注意到，33％的所产生的食物浪费，而世界上有9％的人饥饿或营养不良。这些社会司机与传统技术系统复合，过于缓慢和效率低，无法穿越越来越多的农业供应链。 Agstack将使用协作和开源软件来建立21世纪的数字基础设施，即将成为新应用，效率和规模的创新催化剂。
 “大公司从大型公司爆炸到非营利组织的农业科技创新代表了全球南部和北方农民的比赛变更机，”Agstack Partner Digital Green的联合创始人和执行主任Rikin Gandhi说。 “与此同时，我们构建数字基础设施是至关重要的，确保这些变化的影响使得这些变化的愿望能够成为最边缘化的愿望，并在气候变化中造成弹性，特别是在气候变化中。”
 Agstack由开放式存储库组成，可以创建和发布模型，免费方便地访问公共数据，互操作框架，用于交叉项目使用以及特定于主题的扩展和工具箱。它将利用现有技术，如农业标准（Aggateway，Un-Fao，Cafa，USDA和NASA-AR）;公共数据（Landsat，Sentinel，Noaa和Shordgrids;模型（UC-ANR IPM），以及像超载手，Kubernetes，开放地平线，Postgres，Django等的开源项目。 
“我们很高兴为Agstack提供建设和成长的论坛，”Linux基金会的项目高级经理Mike Dolan说。 “很明显，通过使用开源软件来规范农业的数字基础设施，可降低成本，加速整合并实现创新。看到像农业这样的行业使用开源原则是惊人的创新。“
  Agstack公告本周在Agtech Space中遵循其他主要资金新闻。周二，加拿大农场机器人公司精密AI宣布，它已成功关闭了2000万美元的种子回合，由Googlex联合创始人Tom Chi在一个企业，以及BDC Capital的工业创新风险基金，Fulcrum Global Capital和Golden机会基金。
 资金将用于支持精密AI的颠覆性精确农业平台的进步，部署AI动力无人机的群体，以大大减少行作物农业中的除草剂。
 根据精密AI，除草剂喷涂是最不高效的农业活动之一，在裸地上浪费了80％，另外15％落在作物上。虽然竞争对手专注于高价值，但Precision Ai表示，它的技术可以以比传统的大型农业机械的成本低得多的成本，潜在地将其技术较低，潜在地减少95％，同时保持作物产量和每个不断增长的季节挽救农民每亩高达52美元。
note_en:
On Wednesday,  Linux Foundation, the nonprofit organization that seeks to enable mass innovation through open source technology, announced the launch of the  AgStack Foundation, the first open source digital infrastructure project designed specifically for the  global agriculture ecosystem.
 AgStack Foundation will improve  global agriculture efficiency through the creation, maintenance and enhancement of free, reusable, open and specialized digital infrastructure for farming data and applications.
 Founding members and contributors include leaders from both the  technology and agriculture industries, spanning sectors and geographies. Members and partners include  Agralogics,  Call for Code,  Centricity Global,  Digital Green,  Farm Foundation,  farmOS,  HPE,  IBM,  Mixing Bowl & Better Food Ventures,  NIAB,  OpenTeam,  Our Sci,  Produce Marketing Association,   Purdue University / OATS & Agricultural Informatics Lab, the   University of California Agriculture and Natural Resources (UC-ANR)  and   University of California Santa Barbara SmartFarm Project.
 “The global agriculture ecosystem desperately needs a  digital makeover. There is too much loss of  productivity and innovation due to the absence of re-usable tools and data. I’m excited to lead this community of leaders, contributors and members – from across sectors and countries – to help build this common and re-usable resource – AgStack – that will help every stakeholder in global agriculture with free and open digital tools and data,” said Sumer Johal, Executive Director of AgStack.
 Linux Foundation noted in its official AgStack announcement on Wednesday that 33 percent of all food produced is wasted, while nine percent of the people in the world are hungry or undernourished. These societal drivers are compounded with legacy technology systems that are too slow and inefficient and can’t work across the growing and more complex agricultural supply chain. AgStack will use collaboration and open source software to build a 21st century digital infrastructure that it says will be a catalyst for innovation on new applications, efficiencies and scale.
 “The explosion of  agri-tech innovations from large companies to startups to governments to non-profits represents a game changer for farmers in both the Global South and North,” said  Rikin Gandhi, Co-founder and Executive Director of AgStack partner  Digital Green. “At the same time, it’s critical that we build digital infrastructure that ensures that the impact of these changes enables the aspirations of those most marginalized and builds their resilience, particularly in the midst of climate change.”
 AgStack consists of an open repository to create and publish models, free and easy access to public data, interoperable frameworks for cross-project use and topic-specific extensions and toolboxes. It will leverage existing technologies such as agriculture standards (AgGateway, UN-FAO, CAFA, USDA and NASA-AR); public data (Landsat, Sentinel, NOAA and Soilgrids; models (UC-ANR IPM), and open source projects like Hyperledger, Kubernetes, Open Horizon, Postgres, Django and more.
 “We’re pleased to provide the forum for AgStack to be built and to grow,” said  Mike Dolan, general manager and senior vice president of projects at the Linux Foundation. “It’s clear that by using open source software to standardize the  digital infrastructure for agriculture, that AgStack can reduce cost, accelerate integration and enable innovation. It’s amazing to see industries like agriculture use open source principles to innovate.”
  The AgStack announcement follows other major funding news in the agtech space this week. On Tuesday, Canadian farm robotics company  Precision AI announced that it has successfully closed a $20 million seed round, led by GoogleX co-founder Tom Chi’s  At One Ventures, together with BDC Capital’s  Industrial Innovation Venture Fund,  Fulcrum Global Capital and  Golden Opportunities Fund.
 Funds will be used to support the advancement of Precision AI’s disruptive precision farming platform that deploys swarms of AI-powered drones to dramatically reduce herbicide use in row crop agriculture.
 According to Precision AI, herbicide spraying is one of the least efficient agricultural activities, with over 80 percent wasted on bare ground and another 15 percent falling on the crop.  While competitors have focused on high-value, low acreage crops, Precision AI says its technology can be applied to large acreage crops at a much lower cost than traditional large farming machinery, potentially reducing pesticide use by up to 95% while maintaining crop yield and saving farmers up to  $52 per acre per growing season.
title: Linux Foundation启动Agstack，这是一个开源的Agtech Initiative
title_en: Linux Foundation starts AgStack, an open-source agtech initiative

一个Cyber​​attack强迫了美国最大的流水线之一，在似乎是扰乱脆弱能源基础设施的重大尝试。管道携带精炼汽油和喷气机从德克萨斯到纽约的东海岸。
 系统的运营商殖民地管道在星期五下旬表示，它已关闭其5,500英里的管道，该管道将携带45％的东海岸的燃料供应，以努力在其计算机网络上遏制违规行为。周五早些时候，沿着管道发生了干扰，但目前尚不清楚这是攻击的直接结果。
 每天殖民地的管道运输250万桶，采取精制汽油，柴油燃料和海湾海岸的喷气燃料到纽约港和纽约的主要机场。大多数都进入了主要的储罐，随着大流行抑郁的能源使用，攻击不太可能导致任何立即中断。
 在声明中，该公司表示，它周五学习了它“是网络安全攻击的受害者”，但它没有提供细节。这种攻击可能涉及恶意软件，用于关闭其运营或赎金软件要求支付以解锁计算机文件或系统。
 “作为回应，我们主动地将某些系统脱机遏制威胁，该威胁暂时停止了所有管道运营，并影响了我们的一些。 “公司表示，”公司表示，参考信息技术系统。
 它表示已联系执法和其他联邦机构。 f.b.i.引导此类调查，但关键的基础设施是国土安全部的Cyber​​security和基础设施安全机构的责任。
 到目前为止，燃料价格对燃料价格的影响一直很小，汽油和柴油期货周五纽约商品交易所上涨了1％。纽约州泵在纽约州的普通汽油价格周六由一分钱上升，从2.99美元到3美元。在过去的一周里，随着全球石油价格迅速上涨，汽油价格上涨了6美分。 
“这是一个严肃的问题，”石油价格信息服务的全球能源分析负责人汤姆克罗萨说。 “它可能会咆哮，因为它是该国的颈动画来自墨西哥湾海岸的燃料到纽约。”
 违规行为仅仅是美国计算机网络的两次重大攻击 - 俄罗斯主力智能服务的Solarwinds入侵，另一个反对已归因于中国黑客的微软电子邮件服务 - 已阐述了政府的网络的漏洞公司依赖。
 虽然这两个攻击出现在至少最初就盗窃电子邮件和其他数据时，但入侵的性质创造了“后门”，该专家表示最终可能会对物理基础设施进行攻击。到目前为止，既不被认为导致数据盗窃以外的任何努力。
 上个月的拜登政府宣布对俄罗斯对俄罗斯进行制裁，以获得Solarwinds，预计将在未来几天发出一项行政命令，这将采取措施确保关键基础设施，包括为供应商提供加强为联邦政府提供服务的安全。
 美国长期以来，警告说，俄罗斯植入了电力汽车网络中的恶意代码，而美国几年前将类似的代码放入俄罗斯网格中。
 但是对能源系统的实际攻击很少见。大约十年前，伊朗被归咎于世界上最大的生产商之一的沙特阿美公司的计算机系统袭击，该公司摧毁了30,000台电脑。这种袭击似乎是对伊朗核离心机的美国以色列袭击，并没有影响行动。
 2017年对沙特石化工厂的另一次攻击几乎掀起了一个主要的工业灾难。但它很快关闭，调查人员后来将其归因于俄罗斯黑客。今年，有人短暂地过度控制一个小佛罗里达州的水处理计划，似乎是毒害供应的努力，但迅速停止尝试。
note_en:
A cyberattack forced the shutdown of one of the largest pipelines in the United States, in what appeared to be a significant attempt to disrupt vulnerable energy infrastructure. The pipeline carries refined gasoline and jet fuel up the East Coast from Texas to New York.
 The operator of the system, Colonial Pipeline, said in a statement late Friday that it had shut down its 5,500 miles of pipeline, which it says carries 45 percent of the East Coast’s fuel supplies, in an effort to contain the breach on its computer networks. Earlier Friday, there were disruptions along the pipeline, but it was unclear whether that was a direct result of the attack.
 Colonial’s pipeline transports 2.5 million barrels each day, taking refined gasoline, diesel fuel and jet fuel from the Gulf Coast up to New York Harbor and New York’s major airports. Most of that goes into major storage tanks, and with energy use depressed by the pandemic, the attack was unlikely to cause any immediate disruptions.
 In the statement, the company said that it learned on Friday that it “was the victim of a cybersecurity attack,” but it provided no details. Such an attack could involve malware that shut down its operations or ransomware demanding payment to unlock computer files or systems.
 “In response, we proactively took certain systems offline to contain the threat, which has temporarily halted all pipeline operations, and affected some of our I.T. operations,” the company said, referring to information technology systems.
 It said it had contacted law enforcement and other federal agencies. The F.B.I. leads such investigations, but critical infrastructure is the responsibility of the Department of Homeland Security’s Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency.
 So far the effect on fuel prices has been small, with gasoline and diesel futures rising about 1 percent on the New York Mercantile Exchange on Friday. Prices for regular gasoline at the pump in New York State rose on Saturday by a penny, from $2.99 to $3. Over the past week, gasoline prices have risen nationwide by 6 cents, as global oil prices have risen rapidly.
 “It’s a serious issue,” said Tom Kloza, the global head of energy analysis at Oil Price Information Service. “It could snarl things up because it is the country’s jugular aorta from moving fuel from the Gulf Coast up to New York.”
 The breach comes just months after two major attacks on American computer networks —  the SolarWinds intrusion by Russia’s main intelligence service, and another against a Microsoft email service that has been attributed to Chinese hackers — that have illustrated the vulnerability of the networks on which the government and corporations rely.
 While both of those attacks appeared aimed, at least initially, on the theft of emails and other data, the nature of the intrusions created “back doors” that experts say could ultimately enable attacks on physical infrastructure. So far, neither effort is thought to have led to anything other than data theft.
 The Biden administration  announced sanctions against Russia last month for SolarWinds, and is expected to issue an executive order in the coming days that would take steps to secure critical infrastructure, including requiring enhanced security for vendors providing services to the federal government.
 The United States has long warned that Russia has implanted malicious code in the electric utility networks, and the United States  responded several years ago by putting similar code into the Russian grid.
 But actual attacks on energy systems are rare. About a decade ago, Iran  was blamed for an attack on the computer systems of Saudi Aramco, one of the world’s largest producers, which destroyed 30,000 computers. That attack, which appeared to be in response to the American-Israeli attack on Iran’s nuclear centrifuges, did not affect operations.
 Another attack on  a Saudi petrochemical plant in 2017 nearly set off a major industrial disaster. But it was shut down quickly, and investigators  later attributed it to Russian hackers. This year, someone  briefly took over control of a water treatment plan in a small Florida city, in what appeared to be an effort to poison the supply, but the attempt was quickly halted.
